ssl/TLSVersion/MAXIMUM_SUPPORTED.name: MAXIMUM_SUPPORTED
ssl/TLSVersion/MAXIMUM_SUPPORTED.description: The minimum or maximum supported SSL or TLS version. These are magic constants. Their values don’t reflect the lowest and highest available TLS/SSL versions.

info.excerpt: The minimum or maximum supported SSL or TLS version. These are magic constants. Their values don’t reflect the lowest and highest available TLS/SSL versions.

ssl/SSLContext/maximum_version.name: maximum_version
ssl/SSLContext/maximum_version.description: A TLSVersion enum member representing the highest supported TLS version. The value defaults to TLSVersion.MAXIMUM_SUPPORTED. The attribute is read-only for protocols other than PROTOCOL_TLS, PROTOCOL_TLS_CLIENT, and PROTOCOL_TLS_SERVER. The attributes maximum_version, minimum_version and SSLContext.options all affect the supported SSL and TLS versions of the context. The implementation does not prevent invalid combination. info.excerpt: A TLSVersion enum member representing the highest supported TLS version. The value defaults to TLSVersion.MAXIMUM_SUPPORTED. The attribute is read-only for protocols other than PROTOCOL_TLS, PROTOCOL_...

ssl/SSLContext/minimum_version.name: minimum_version
ssl/SSLContext/minimum_version.description: Like SSLContext.maximum_version except it is the lowest supported version or TLSVersion.MINIMUM_SUPPORTED. Added in version 3.7.

info.excerpt: Like SSLContext.maximum_version except it is the lowest supported version or TLSVersion.MINIMUM_SUPPORTED. Added in version 3.7.

ssl/MemoryBIO.name: MemoryBIO
ssl/MemoryBIO.description: A memory buffer that can be used to pass data between Python and an SSL protocol instance. pending¶ Return the number of bytes currently in the memory buffer. eof¶ A boolean indicating whether the memory BIO is current at the end-of-file position. read(n=-1, /)¶ Read up to n bytes from the memory buffer.
